title: The One With Joeys Porsche

jokes.text: When? After the birth of our first secret child?
jokes.setup: Ross was going to tell Rachel about still being married
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic escalation to absurd timeline - having secret children
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcastic escalation with perfect economy. The absurd timeline progression lands well and shows Rachel's anger through wit.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, what? You're still married?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, technically yes, but—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God. So what, we've been dating and you're married?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's not like that, I can explain—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, no, I get it. And I'm sure you have secret children too. What are their names? Where do they go to school?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, come on—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Do they know about me? Have you told them mommy's a homewrecker? Should I start a college fund?

jokes.text: At what point did you think this was a successful marriage?
jokes.setup: Ross claims he couldn't face another failed marriage
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out the obvious - their marriage was never successful
jokes.explanation: Solid character-driven sarcasm that cuts right to the absurdity of Ross's logic. Well-structured comeback.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, you couldn't face another failed marriage? We weren't even successful the first time!

jokes.text: Maybe it's best not to think about it.
jokes.setup: Ross suggests the situation is funny if you think about it
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry response suggesting the opposite
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response. Not brilliant but functional deadpan that works for the scene.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, if you think about it, it is kind of funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it's really not.

jokes.text: Another lie. You have a sickness!
jokes.setup: Ross told Phoebe that Rachel already knew about the marriage
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic overreaction calling it a sickness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe character moment - dramatic escalation that's both absurd and somehow logical for her worldview.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: A sickness?! Ross, this is a sickness! She knew?! And she didn't tell me?! Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God! This is betrayal! This is... this is a sickness!

jokes.text: For my own peace of mind, are you married to any more of us?
jokes.setup: Discovery that Ross and Rachel are still married
jokes.punchline: Monica's paranoid but logical extension to check if Ross has married other friends
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect economy. Takes the situation to its logical absurd conclusion while maintaining Monica's character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. If you two are still married, then... oh my God, Ross, who else have you married?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? No one!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Are you sure? Because at this point, I wouldn't be surprised if you married Chandler too.

jokes.text: I make 4 bucks an hour. I saved up for 350 years.
jokes.setup: Joey claiming the Porsche keys are his
jokes.punchline: Absurd math about how long he'd need to save on his salary
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey math joke - absurd but delivered with perfect deadpan confidence. Great character integration showing his financial situation and logic.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: These are my keys. I'm buying a Porsche.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: On what salary?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna save up. I figure if I save $1000 a month, in about... 400 years, I'll have enough for a down payment.

jokes.text: I didn't realize till I got home. I wasn't gonna walk back with one shoe.
jokes.setup: Gunther left a shoe at Central Perk
jokes.punchline: His logical reasoning for not coming back immediately
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about a relatable situation. Fits Gunther's understated character well.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Well, I figured if I didn't come back right away, maybe someone would try it on and it would fit perfectly, and they'd think, 'Wow, whoever lost this shoe has impeccable taste.' And then maybe they'd wait around for me to come back, and we'd fall in love.

jokes.text: By the way, they're not actually puppies. They're Frank and Alice's triplets.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks them to take care of three cute puppies
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actually human babies
jokes.explanation: Well-executed misdirection that works perfectly for Phoebe's character. Good structure with clear setup and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I need you to take care of these three cute puppies for me.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Puppies? Phoebe, these are human babies!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, I know. But they're easier to say yes to if you think they're puppies.

jokes.text: I'm gonna pass, because I was kind of iffy when it was puppies.
jokes.setup: Learning they need to babysit human babies instead of puppies
jokes.punchline: Chandler's honest admission he wasn't even interested in puppies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response - self-aware and honest about his limitations. Good callback to the puppy setup.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I mean, let's be honest here. I wasn't even interested in the puppies.

jokes.text: It'll give us great practice for when... people with babies come to visit.
jokes.setup: Monica starting to say practice for when they have babies
jokes.punchline: Awkward correction to avoid assuming their relationship future
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica catching herself before being presumptuous about their relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We should practice for when we have babies.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: When we have babies?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I mean, if we have babies. If.

jokes.text: Nice car. / It's not mine. / I love your car. / Yeah. It's mine.
jokes.setup: Woman compliments the car, Joey denies ownership
jokes.punchline: Immediately changes tune when she expresses love for it
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey opportunism shown perfectly through immediate contradiction based on the woman's interest level.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Wow, is this your car?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, it's not mine.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: It's really nice.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it's not really... I mean, it's okay.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: I love it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's mine. I love it too!

jokes.text: It's got them!
jokes.setup: Woman asks if he likes leather seats
jokes.punchline: Joey's enthusiastic confirmation despite not actually knowing
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment showing his willingness to fake knowledge to impress women.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Do you like leather seats?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, absolutely. Love 'em.

jokes.text: December 26th. Maybe she's Santa Claus.
jokes.setup: Denise said she'd be back December 26th
jokes.punchline: Monica's silly conclusion about Santa Claus
jokes.explanation: Mild joke that relies more on randomness than craft. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, so Denise is coming back December 26th? That means Santa Claus is real and he's just really bad at managing his schedule.

jokes.text: Oh, look who it is. My husband, the apple of my eye.
jokes.setup: Rachel is angry at Ross about the marriage
jokes.punchline: Overly sweet sarcastic greeting
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic delivery that establishes Rachel's anger while maintaining humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh hi Ross! How are you doing today? I'm so glad to see you. You look absolutely wonderful. I love you so much.

jokes.text: Sure, if you say you'll take care of it, I have no reason to doubt you.
jokes.setup: Ross offers to take care of the annulment forms
jokes.punchline: Heavy sarcasm referencing his track record of follow-through
jokes.explanation: Strong sarcasm that references the established conflict. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're going to take care of the annulment forms? Oh great, because you have such a good track record with follow-through.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What do you mean? I always follow through on things.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? When did you finish that bookshelf you started three months ago?

jokes.text: Okay, Rach, but-- / You're peeping!
jokes.setup: Rachel said she doesn't want to hear a peep
jokes.punchline: Calls Ross out for making any sound at all
jokes.explanation: Quick callback with good economy. Takes the peep metaphor literally for comedic effect.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't want to hear a peep out of you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What was that?

jokes.text: Oh, I am so mad, Ross! I don't think I've ever been this angry!
jokes.setup: Rachel's building anger about the marriage situation
jokes.punchline: Claims this is the angriest she's ever been
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke. Functional escalation but not particularly clever on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This is the angriest I've ever been!

jokes.text: What about when I said we were on a break?
jokes.setup: Rachel claims she's never been this angry
jokes.punchline: Ross references their most famous fight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to the show's most famous recurring argument. Perfect character moment and highly quotable. Excellent comedic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I've never been this angry.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh please, you should've seen you when you found out I slept with someone else while we were on a break.

jokes.text: Little Baby Girl Chandler. Where have I heard that before? Oh, Coach Rubin.
jokes.setup: Monica calls the baby Little Baby Girl Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler remembers being called this himself by a coach
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating Chandler moment that references his character history. Well-constructed revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. Little Baby Girl Chandler? That's what Coach Hernandez used to call me in high school!

jokes.text: We should really be playing a zone defense. / Things'll go smoother if we each have a zone.
jokes.setup: Struggling with babysitting three babies
jokes.punchline: Applies sports strategy to childcare
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist approach that makes logical sense. Good Chandler voice and clever metaphor extension.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm gonna try a zone defense. Joey, you take the left side, I'll take the right, and Monica, you're in the middle.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, these are babies, not the Knicks!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, the Knicks strategy might actually work better on them.

jokes.text: I'll be in charge of looking at how cute they are when they put their hands... / Sounds great, but you should be in charge of wiping.
jokes.setup: Chandler assigns himself the easy job of just watching
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately reassigns him to diaper duty
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Chandler trying to avoid work and Monica taking charge.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you're on diaper duty.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? But I just got the easy job of watching!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Not anymore. Diaper duty. Now.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm a rookie. I should not be in the end zone.
jokes.setup: Being assigned diaper duty
jokes.punchline: Continues sports metaphor by calling diaper area 'end zone'
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of the sports metaphor with good double meaning. Maintains Chandler's character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more in the end zone right now?

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, this is not what I ordered.
jokes.setup: Monica comparing baby care to restaurant plate setting
jokes.punchline: Treating the baby situation like a restaurant complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character moment extending her restaurant management mindset to baby care.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so you're saying the baby situation is like a restaurant complaint?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if a customer comes in and says their plate is dirty, I don't just wipe it off and serve them again. I get them a new plate, I apologize, and I make sure it never happens again.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So maybe that's what we need to do here.

jokes.text: Shocking, since you still have the keys.
jokes.setup: Joey amazed the Porsche is still there
jokes.punchline: Monica points out the obvious reason
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response pointing out Joey's obliviousness. Solid economy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't believe the Porsche is still here!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's because nobody wants it, Joey. It's parked in our garage.

jokes.text: What equity investments? / The ones that got me the Porsche. Will you keep up?
jokes.setup: Joey mentions equity investments guy advised him on
jokes.punchline: Joey's circular logic about nonexistent investments and condescending tone
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic - completely nonsensical but delivered with absolute confidence. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, this equity investments guy told me I should diversify my portfolio.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, you don't have any investments.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Exactly! Which is why I need to diversify them.

jokes.text: I know. Once there was a dirty car in front of the building, so I washed it. / And? / And six others.
jokes.setup: Monica has extensive car washing supplies despite no car
jokes.punchline: Reveals she got carried away and washed multiple random cars
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment showing her compulsive cleaning taken to absurd extremes. Great escalation structure.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I like to be prepared!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you don't have a car.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but I got really into it. I may have washed some cars in the parking lot.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Some cars?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, a lot of cars. And they weren't all parked there.

jokes.text: But it's my car. / Yeah, but it's my wax.
jokes.setup: Car owner wants Joey to stop polishing his car
jokes.punchline: Joey's ridiculous logic that his wax gives him rights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic with perfect economy. Joey's confidence in completely flawed reasoning is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Car Owner
dialogue.line: Stop polishing my car!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But I waxed it. My wax, my rules.

jokes.text: Yeah, two hours, a lifetime, that's the same.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying babysitting is easy after two hours
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic equation of their brief experience with full-time parenting
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with good economy. Points out the obvious flaw in Phoebe's reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yes, two hours of babysitting. That's basically the same as raising a child for eighteen years. You should definitely have more kids.

jokes.text: That thing could put someone's eye out. / He can do more than that. He can destroy the universe.
jokes.setup: Monica worried about toy safety
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic escalation about the toy's universe-destroying power
jokes.explanation: Good escalation taking Monica's safety concern to absurd cosmic levels. Well-structured buildup.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, is this toy safe? There's no safety label.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Not safe? Monica, this toy doesn't just break — it opens a portal to another dimension.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And not a fun dimension. A dimension where everyone's a tax accountant and it's always February.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: In fact, I'm pretty sure if you wind it up backwards, it unwinds the entire fabric of spacetime.

jokes.text: Also, look at his smooth area. That's just gonna mess them up.
jokes.setup: Monica examining the action figure for child safety
jokes.punchline: Her concern about the anatomical correctness affecting children
jokes.explanation: Mildly uncomfortable adult humor disguised as child safety concern. Fits Monica's overprotective character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, this is anatomically correct!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And that's a problem because...?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because it's a children's toy! What if a kid looks at this and thinks that's what they're supposed to look like?

jokes.text: When I was a kid, my mom used to throw me into a pile of broken glass. / What? / Glass, sand, whatever.
jokes.setup: Disagreeing about overprotective parenting
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual mention of childhood abuse, then casual correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe dark humor delivered with perfect casualness. The casual correction makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My mother used to lock me in the closet.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, actually the cabinet. It was a really small closet.

jokes.text: I wonder when you stop putting both legs behind your head. / I can still do that. / How are you still single?
jokes.setup: Monica wondering about babies' flexibility, Phoebe reveals she's still flexible
jokes.punchline: Chandler's incredulous question about her dating life
jokes.explanation: Well-structured three-part joke with good escalation. Chandler's reaction is perfectly timed and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just can't believe how flexible babies are.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm still flexible!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How is that possible?

jokes.text: I swallowed his sonic blaster gun. / How did that happen? / I was trying to prove that I was right.
jokes.setup: Chandler swallowed part of the toy
jokes.punchline: His explanation that he was trying to prove the toy was safe
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the toy safety debate. Chandler's commitment to being right backfires spectacularly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I was trying to prove the toy was safe!

jokes.text: This whole time we were concentrating on the babies... and no one was watching Chandler!
jokes.setup: Discovery that Chandler swallowed the toy piece
jokes.punchline: Realization that Chandler needed babysitting too
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reframes the entire situation. Great revelation that Chandler is the biggest child.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, so Chandler swallowed it?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, he swallowed the little piece.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Chandler needs a babysitter too!

jokes.text: What? / Yes. Heroin and crack. / Crack isn't even an intravenous drug. / Well, you would know.
jokes.setup: Ross shocked about being called a drug user on legal forms
jokes.punchline: Rachel's quick comeback suggesting his knowledge proves guilt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant quick comeback that turns Ross's correction against him. Perfect example of escalating absurd accusations.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't believe they're asking if I use drugs on this legal form!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, the fact that you know all the street names is kind of suspicious.

jokes.text: Ross, please. I found the magazines.
jokes.setup: Ross protesting he didn't lie about sexual preference
jokes.punchline: Rachel's fake evidence about finding gay magazines
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake accusations with convincing fake evidence delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not gay!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh really? Then explain these.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I found these under your bed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Those aren't mine!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Because they have your name written inside them.

jokes.text: Well, that makes sense since you're gay and addicted to heroin.
jokes.setup: Judge reading that Ross was unable to consummate marriage
jokes.punchline: Rachel's deadpan logical connection of all the false accusations
jokes.explanation: Excellent culmination bringing together all the absurd accusations into logical conclusion.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so let me get this straight. He couldn't... and she's saying he did... with her?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So basically, the one thing he couldn't do, is the one thing she's accusing him of doing?

jokes.text: I'll consummate this marriage right here, right now!
jokes.setup: Ross protesting he has no consummation problems
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate offer to prove himself in court
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross escalation - inappropriate but completely in character. Great cringe comedy moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I do not have a problem!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Because I heard—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I can prove it right now if you want. We could go to the courthouse, get a marriage license, and I could consummate it right there on the judge's bench!

jokes.text: And when we were dating, we consummated like bunnies.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to defend his sexual abilities in court
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed and inappropriate courtroom testimony
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross overshare moment. The phrase 'like bunnies' in legal context is perfectly cringe.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Your Honor, I object to the characterization that I am, in any way, inadequate in the bedroom. In fact, I have extensive experience with a wide variety of... techniques and positions.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Mr. Geller, this is a custody hearing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, well, I'm just saying that my flexibility and stamina are beyond reproach. I once lasted forty-five minutes without stopping, and that was just the foreplay.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: We don't need to hear this.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But Your Honor, the opposing counsel implied—

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Mr. Geller, sit down. Now.

jokes.text: Strike 'consummated like bunnies' from the record.
jokes.setup: Ross's inappropriate courtroom testimony
jokes.punchline: Court reporter's professional but pointed response
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat from supporting character. Professional response to inappropriate situation.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was in the zone. I was just... I was on fire.

dialogue.speaker: Court Reporter
dialogue.line: You were on fire in a courtroom during a deposition?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Metaphorically!

jokes.text: Well, yes. We got married in Vegas. / And the names, I think.
jokes.setup: Judge asks if anything on the form is actually true
jokes.punchline: Rachel's minimal admission of what's actually factual
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan acknowledgment of how few true facts remain on their form. The uncertainty about names is extra funny.

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Is anything on this form actually true?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, my name is Rachel.

jokes.text: Stop typing! / Stop typing, stop typing!
jokes.setup: Ross frustrated that court reporter keeps typing despite dismissal
jokes.punchline: His increasingly frantic demands that become self-defeating
jokes.explanation: Great escalating frustration that becomes self-defeating. Perfect Ross meltdown moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no! Stop typing! The court is dismissed!

dialogue.speaker: Court Reporter
dialogue.line: I'm just getting down the dismissal, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well stop! Every word you type is part of the record!

dialogue.speaker: Court Reporter
dialogue.line: Exactly, so I need to—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No! Don't type that! You're making it worse! Stop talking about the typing while you're typing about the typing!

jokes.text: Okay, do you see what you're keeping me married to?
jokes.setup: Ross's courtroom meltdown
jokes.punchline: Rachel using his behavior as evidence for why she wants divorce
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel response that frames Ross's behavior as evidence for her case. Self-aware commentary.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Your Honor, I rest my case. He's clearly unstable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I am NOT unstable!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You just yelled at a judge about a sandwich.

jokes.text: Lady, here's the deal. I came here for an annulment, and I'm not leaving here until I get one! / Would you like a night in jail? / Thank you for your time.
jokes.setup: Rachel's defiant stance toward the judge
jokes.punchline: Immediate backdown when threatened with jail
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation. Rachel's tough stance immediately crumbles at first real consequence. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not backing down. I know my rights!

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.line: Ms. Green, if you don't comply, I'll hold you in contempt of court.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, actually, I think I was wrong. You're totally right. I back down. I back down so much.

jokes.text: But you found the keys to his clothes?
jokes.setup: Joey explaining the Porsche owner took his car back
jokes.punchline: Monica's logical question about why he's dressed like a Porsche owner
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor pointing out the absurdity of Joey's costume choice.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, if he took the car back, why are you still dressed like a Porsche owner?

jokes.text: Of course. Only an idiot would wear this stuff if he didn't have the car. / That is true.
jokes.setup: Joey's logic about Porsche clothes meaning Porsche ownership
jokes.punchline: Monica's agreement despite Joey proving the opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment where Joey accidentally describes himself while Monica misses the obvious contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: If you wear Porsche clothes, you own a Porsche.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're wearing a Porsche shirt.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So you own a Porsche!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I do not own a Porsche.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then you can't wear the shirt.

jokes.text: Yeah, but only a genius would swallow a sonic blaster gun. / Oh, I've been there.
jokes.setup: Joey's sarcastic comment about Chandler's mishap
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual admission she's done something similar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - casual admission of bizarre past experience delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh wow, that's a real smooth move, Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I did that once. Except it was with a turkey and a mailbox.

jokes.text: You know you don't actually have one. / What are you doing? I'm in character. Will you talk to her?
jokes.setup: Monica pointing out Joey doesn't have a Porsche
jokes.punchline: Joey's commitment to his fake persona, treating it like acting method
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where Joey applies his acting training to his deception. Character-perfect response.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You don't have a Porsche, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but when I'm out there, I'm not Joey Tribbiani. I'm the guy who has the Porsche.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: But you don't have a Porsche.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, it's called method acting. I'm committed to the character.

jokes.text: But a doctor won't be able to help him. And it's just gonna... naturally pass through his system in seven years. / I think that's gum. / I'm pretty sure it's gun.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Chandler's swallowed toy piece
jokes.punchline: Confusion between gum digestion myth and gun/weapon wordplay
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke mixing common myth with weapon terminology. Fits Phoebe's character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So Chandler swallowed a piece of a toy?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and they said it'll pass through his system in like seven years.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Seven years? That's the gum thing!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, this is different. This is a toy. Although, you know, gum, toys... they're both gonna come out the same way.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that's not how it works.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, like you know everything about digestion!

jokes.text: No! What would Krog do?
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to convince Chandler not to go to hospital
jokes.punchline: Asking what the action figure would do in medical situation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment applying action figure logic to real medical situation. Perfectly fits Phoebe's character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You can't go to the hospital! Hospitals are where people go to die.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, a lot of people go to get better.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's what they tell you. But what if you had an action figure? What would the action figure do?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The action figure would go to the hospital.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Exactly! Because it doesn't have a choice!

jokes.text: Maybe because you've got the keys?
jokes.setup: Joey wondering why valet hasn't returned with 'his' Porsche
jokes.punchline: Joey realizing the obvious reason - he has the keys
jokes.explanation: Simple realization joke. Joey finally grasping obvious logic. Functional but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Where is my car? I gave it to the valet like twenty minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... I have the keys.

jokes.text: Did a Porsche throw up on you?
jokes.setup: Joey wearing ridiculous Porsche-branded clothing
jokes.punchline: Monica's creative description of his over-the-top outfit
jokes.explanation: Creative visual description with good economy. The 'throw up' metaphor perfectly captures excess of branded clothing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wow, Joey! You look like a Porsche threw up on you!

jokes.text: It's 'Porsche-a'!
jokes.setup: Monica's comment about Porsche
jokes.punchline: Joey's pretentious mispronunciation attempting to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment trying to sound sophisticated but getting it wrong. Good character-driven pronunciation humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's not a Porsche, it's a Porsch-ay.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I didn't mean to scare you. I just have to clean that up. Because, let's face it, we're at Monica's.
jokes.setup: Phoebe apologizing to babies for sudden movement
jokes.punchline: Explanation that Monica's standards require immediate cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good character reference showing everyone's awareness of Monica's cleaning standards. Natural delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm so sorry! Did I startle you? Mommy's just moving a little bit.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, they're not real babies.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, but I have to practice. And besides, with your standards, if I don't treat these dolls right, you're gonna make me clean them.

jokes.text: I broke it. All right. Well, that's just the way things go.
jokes.setup: Phoebe breaks something in Monica's apartment
jokes.punchline: Her casual acceptance of what would normally be a big deal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's zen-like acceptance contrasting with Monica's usual panic over breakage.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oops, I broke your vase.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: WHAT?! That was an antique!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, these things happen. I once broke a guy's leg and he was totally fine with it.

jokes.text: Now would be a good time for your first words.
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes one baby is missing
jokes.punchline: Asking the babies to help solve the crisis by talking
jokes.explanation: Good desperation humor with logical-but-impossible request. Shows Phoebe's panic through absurd hope.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, one of you guys has to tell me where the other one is. Come on, talk to me. Please? One of you talk!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I know you can do it. I've heard you guys... you know, making noises and stuff.

jokes.text: Let's just say, Krog will be equipped to destroy the universe again... in 12 to 14 hours.
jokes.setup: Asked how he feels after hospital visit
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic explanation of when toy piece will pass through his system
jokes.explanation: Excellent euphemistic callback that maintains the toy's mythical status while discussing bodily functions. Great Chandler voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, the doctor says it should pass through my system in a couple of days. So I guess I'll just have to be patient.

jokes.text: And protected them from a tornado?
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming she took care of babies successfully
jokes.punchline: Monica's observation about the destruction in her apartment
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag description. Monica's tornado metaphor perfectly captures the apartment's destruction while maintaining her character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm great with babies! I've taken care of like a hundred of them.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, look at my apartment. It looks like a tornado hit it. There's baby food on the ceiling. The ceiling, Phoebe!

jokes.text: Was the setting of Phoebe's triumph.
jokes.setup: Monica upset about her destroyed apartment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's diplomatic reframing of the disaster as victory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler diplomatic humor. Reframes disaster as triumph with elegant understatement. Great supportive friend moment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My apartment is destroyed!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Look at the bright side - at least now you have an open floor plan.

jokes.text: And I thought it would be funnier if we got married. / So, as a compromise, we decided first to get married and then... to eat a lot of grapes.
jokes.setup: Rachel revealing her role in the Vegas marriage
jokes.punchline: The absurd compromise between eating grapes and getting married
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor showing drunk logic. The compromise between two unrelated activities is perfectly absurd and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I got married in Vegas!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?! When?!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Last night! It was so romantic, we were eating grapes and—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You got married over grapes?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, we couldn't decide between eating grapes or getting married, so we did both!

jokes.text: I gotta say... I know I divorce a lot of women. / Never thought I'd be divorcing you.
jokes.setup: Ross reflecting on the divorce
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his serial divorce pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment. Ross acknowledging his pattern while showing genuine emotion about Rachel. Great character development.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, I know. Three divorces. I'm basically the Elizabeth Taylor of my generation. Except, you know, without the talent, the beauty, the successful film career, or the ability to keep a marriage together.

jokes.text: And we wouldn't have our wedding dinner at Pizza Hut. / Did I even treat? / No, it was on the house. It was a newlywed special.
jokes.setup: Rachel imagining what a real wedding would have been like
jokes.punchline: Revelation that even their sad Pizza Hut dinner was free
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating sadness about their wedding. Each detail gets progressively more pathetic, ending with the ultimate indignity.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can picture it now. A beautiful ceremony, white roses, string quartet...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And then we'd go to this amazing reception at the Plaza Hotel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Five-course meal, champagne, dancing all night...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, it would have been perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what? Even that sad Pizza Hut dinner we had was free.

jokes.text: That may be the most depressing thing I've heard.
jokes.setup: Learning their wedding dinner was a free newlywed special
jokes.punchline: Rachel's understated reaction to the pathetic revelation
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction that perfectly captures the accumulating sadness. Rachel's delivery would sell this well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We got the free newlywed special.

jokes.text: Hi, Joey. / Hey. How you doing? / He has the most amazing Porsche under there. I'd show you, but I just tucked her in. She's sleeping.
jokes.setup: Woman greeting Joey near the covered car
jokes.punchline: Joey treating the car like a sleeping child, maintaining his fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to Joey's arc. His commitment to the fantasy reaches absurd new heights, treating the car like a baby. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Shh, shh, shh. She's sleeping.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: It's a car.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know what it is. And right now, she's sleeping. So we're gonna be very, very quiet, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Joey, it's just a —

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Shh! You're gonna wake her up!

tmdb_overview: Ross and Rachel try to get an annulment, but run into some legal obstacles. Joey finds keys to a Porsche in Central Perk and discovers some perks of being a fancy car owner. Meanwhile, Chandler, Monica and Phoebe babysit the triplets.
insight: Joey's fake-rich persona sustains 64 jokes across 21 minutes of escalating deception.
